title: SBC President Slanders SBC Pastor
author: Rachel Burkey
orientation: Rachel Burkey recounts a September 2022 Twitter exchange in which SBC President Bart Barber publicly attacked SBC pastor and abolitionist Dusty Deevers for criticizing ERLC appointee Brent Leatherwood, and uses the episode to defend abolitionist legislation against pro-life objections about coercion, ectopic pregnancy, and criminalization.
audiences: Southern Baptists weighing the Barber/Deevers dispute
Pro-life Christians considering the abolitionist position
Skeptics worried abolition bills will jail coerced women
Lawmakers and policy staff evaluating equal protection bills

questions.question: What specific tweet from Dusty Deevers triggered the public dispute with Bart Barber?
questions.audience: Southern Baptists weighing the Barber/Deevers dispute
questions.answer: On September 14, 2022, Deevers tweeted that Brent Leatherwood, newly leading the ERLC, had previously sided against the preborn in Louisiana and at the SBC annual meeting, and lamented that the SBC glories in its shame for elevating him.
questions.quote: In Louisiana this spring and at SBC annual meeting this summer, Brent Leatherwood sided against the innocent preborn now he leads the ethics and policy arm of the SBC. We glory in our shame.

questions.question: How did SBC President Bart Barber characterize Deevers' tweet in his response?
questions.audience: Southern Baptists weighing the Barber/Deevers dispute
questions.answer: Barber quote-tweeted Deevers the next morning, calling the tweet a low point of dishonesty driven by secular political tone rather than the way of Christ.
questions.quote: This tweet represents the lowest point of dishonesty to which we can descend when we sell out to the crass tone of secular politics instead of following the way of Christ.

questions.question: What concrete action did Brent Leatherwood take that Deevers was referring to?
questions.audience: Lawmakers and policy staff evaluating equal protection bills
questions.answer: In May 2022, Leatherwood signed an open letter from pro-life organizations and leaders asking pro-life legislators to oppose HB813, the Abolition of Abortion in Louisiana Act, which would have criminalized abortion as murder.
questions.quote: In May of this year, Leatherwood joined the effort to kill [HB813](https://freethestates.org/2022/05/abolition-of-abortion-in-louisiana-act-passes-committee/index.html), (the Abolition of Abortion in Louisiana act) via an [open letter](https://freethestates.org/www.nrlc.org/uploads/communications/051222coalitionlettertostates1e28.pdf?fbclid=IwAR3gJmBpnoSFll0eQCzRC9h2PzDtBbdTEH-p4HKLHNP8WSeuhDSiVCz-mtg) from Pro-life organizations and leaders that pleaded with pro-life legislators to oppose all legislation that criminalized abortion as murder

questions.question: Does Burkey concede any point Barber made about Deevers?
questions.audience: Pro-life Christians considering the abolitionist position
questions.answer: Yes. Burkey grants Barber's observation that Deevers is attacking the historical pro-life position of the SBC, because Deevers is openly an abolitionist who sees the pro-life and abolitionist movements as theologically opposed.
questions.quote: Barber states that Deevers is attacking the historical pro-life position of the Southern Baptists. This is true. Deevers is not pro-life: he is an abolitionist and an exemplary leader within the movement at that.

questions.question: How does the article answer Barber's worry that abolition laws would imprison coerced 16-year-old girls?
questions.audience: Skeptics worried abolition bills will jail coerced women
questions.answer: Burkey says abolition bills complement existing duress and coercion laws that protect those who commit crimes under coercion and prosecute the aggressors, so the fear is misplaced and Barber's framing is dishonest.
questions.quote: He brings up coerced abortions, ignorant to or ignoring how an abolition bill complements the coercion laws we already have on the books: laws protecting those who commit a crime under duress, and prosecuting the aggressors.

questions.question: How does Burkey respond to Barber's invocation of ectopic pregnancy?
questions.audience: Pro-life Christians considering the abolitionist position
questions.answer: She argues that current homicide laws already require medical professionals to treat all humans with dignity and save as many lives as possible, and the same protections that keep adults from being murdered by doctors should equally apply to image-bearers in the womb and in fallopian tubes.
questions.quote: The current laws that protect Barber from being murdered by a medical professional should equally protect image-bearers of God in utero and in fallopian tubes from murder, and where needed, spur on research towards sparing those lives.

questions.question: What logical fallacies did Grace Family Baptist say Barber's thread contained?
questions.audience: Southern Baptists weighing the Barber/Deevers dispute
questions.answer: Grace Family Baptist tweeted that Barber's thread crammed in appeal to authority, hyperbole, no true Scotsman, straw man, hasty generalization, anecdotal evidence, Texas sharpshooter, and personal incredulity, and added that the worst part was Ninth Commandment violations.
questions.quote: How many logical fallacies can you cram into one tweet thread: appeal to authority, hyperbole, no true scotsman, straw man, hasty generalization, anecdotal evidence, Texas sharpshooter, and personal incredulity. But, the worst part is the Ninth Commandment violations.

questions.question: What attempts have abolitionists made to engage Barber after the exchange?
questions.answer: Deevers and attorney Bradley Pierce both individually asked Barber to talk, dozens of SBC Christians and pastors have urged him to repent, Pierce offered written corrections on legislation, and Deevers and Pierce hosted a roundtable on September 20 that Barber was invited to but could not attend due to the SBC Executive Committee meeting.
questions.quote: Since this offense, Deevers and Bradley Pierce – an attorney and abolitionist – have both individually asked Barber to have a conversation with them. Barber has been confronted by dozens of Christians and pastors throughout the SBC, explaining to him where he’s wrong, and exhorting him to repent and apologize to Deevers for his error.

questions.question: How does Burkey distinguish wanting abortion to end from wanting it abolished?
questions.audience: Pro-life Christians considering the abolitionist position
questions.answer: She says pro-lifers and abolitionists alike pray for an end to abortion, but pro-lifers oppose abolishing it, where abolish is a legal term meaning to do away with the practice by criminalizing it as murder under the same laws that protect any other person.
questions.quote: Abolish is a word describing legal action, and it means to do away with a system or practice: in this case, the practice of modern-day child sacrifice. To do away with this practice, it must be illegal – that is, criminalized as murder.

questions.question: Why does Burkey argue criminalization, not regulation, is necessary to actually stop abortions?
questions.audience: Lawmakers and policy staff evaluating equal protection bills
questions.answer: She appeals to the wickedness of the human heart and Romans 13, arguing that as long as a mother can murder her child without consequence she will, and that the threat of prosecution would deter selfish, murder-minded parents in a way that pro-life regulation cannot.
questions.quote: As long as a mother can murder her own baby without consequences, she will. Murder-minded parents are selfish. And because their main concern is self, the possibility of facing prosecution for the murder of their child would lead them away from murdering their child.

questions.question: What closing exhortation does Burkey leave for Christians who still oppose criminalizing abortion?
questions.audience: Pro-life Christians considering the abolitionist position
questions.answer: She calls it a grave error, signaling bigotry toward preborn neighbors, lack of love for abortion-minded parents, disregard for God's commands, and endorsement of what God calls an abomination, while urging readers to test her claims against Scripture themselves.
questions.quote: It is a grave error to oppose criminalizing abortion as murder. This indicates [bigotry towards our preborn neighbors](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=QYFDuNWmqTE&t=2s), lack of [love for abortion-minded parents](https://fb.watch/fEY6IFtKWY/), lack of care for what [God commands](https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLScgVHlX3klYqQthBEJ-6YGgANtFuNaLjYXiQ6loUzaHOLu8OA/viewform), and an endorsement of what God calls an [abomination](https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Proverbs%2017%3A15&version=ESV).

related.title: SBC President Bart Barber Gives a Lesson in How Not to Repent
related.reason: Direct sequel covering Barber's later non-apology to Deevers.

related.title: Abolition of Abortion in Louisiana Act Passes Committee!
related.reason: The HB813 bill Leatherwood worked to kill, which is the historical context for Deevers' tweet.

related.title: Rep. Alan Seabaugh and the Louisiana House Ensure Murder by Abortion Continues
related.reason: Documents the gutting of the same Louisiana abolition bill the open letter targeted.

related.title: Southern Baptists of Texas Testifiers Go Off the Rails While Defending Incrementalism
related.reason: Earlier piece featuring Bart Barber defending incrementalism against abolition legislation.

related.title: Against Pro-Life Compromise: Responding to Denny Burk, Andrew Walker, et al.
related.reason: Addresses the same SBC pro-life objections to abolition that Barber raised, including ectopic pregnancy.

related.title: The Rise and Progress of Abortion Abolitionism in the Southern Baptist Convention
related.reason: Background on Deevers and the SBC abolitionist movement that Barber was responding to.

related.title: Equal Protection
related.reason: Foundational explainer on the criminalization-as-murder position Burkey defends.
